page: UpCloud GPU-24xCPU-480GB-2xH100 is a GPU-accelerated server instance designed for intensive computational workloads. It features 24 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8462Y+ processors running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The server is equipped with 480.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 20.0 GB per core. Acceleration is delivered via two NVIDIA Hopper H100 GPUs, providing a combined 160 GB of VRAM. This instance does not include local storage, meaning storage must be attached externally.

Benchmark data indicates strong performance across multiple vectors. The single-core CPU performance via stress-ng and PassMark CPU tests ranks in the top 25%, while multi-core performance is average. Memory bandwidth for both small and large blocks is strong, and single-threaded compression and decompression tasks (such as gzip and bzip3) achieve top-tier status in the top 10%. For AI workloads, LLM inference speed using a small 135M model reaches top-tier performance for both prompt processing and text generation.

A key architectural tradeoff is the shared CPU allocation, which may introduce variability under sustained multi-core CPU loads, as reflected in the average multi-core benchmark results. However, the high density of system memory and massive GPU VRAM make it a highly cost-efficient platform for memory-intensive GPU workloads compared to provisioning larger CPU-only clusters. This server is best suited for large-scale machine learning, LLM inference, data science pipelines, and high-performance computing tasks that rely heavily on GPU acceleration rather than sustained multi-core CPU execution.
description: UpCloud GPU-24xCPU-480GB-2xH100 is a GPU-accelerated server designed for high-performance computing and machine learning. It features 24 vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8462Y+ processor, 480.0 GB of system memory, and two NVIDIA Hopper H100 GPUs with a total of 160 GB VRAM. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under KVM virtualization, the instance utilizes a shared CPU allocation and includes no local storage. Benchmarks show top-tier performance in single-threaded compression and LLM inference for small models, alongside strong memory bandwidth and single-core CPU execution. While the shared CPU model presents a potential tradeoff for sustained multi-core CPU workloads, the massive GPU and memory capacity provides excellent resource density. This server is highly suited for deep learning, LLM inference, data science, and memory-intensive GPU-accelerated workloads.
og_description: UpCloud GPU-24xCPU-480GB-2xH100 is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 24 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 480 GB RAM, and two NVIDIA H100 GPUs with 160 GB VRAM, optimized for machine learning and LLM inference.
meta_description: UpCloud GPU-24xCPU-480GB-2xH100 features 24 Intel vCPUs, 480 GB RAM, and two NVIDIA H100 GPUs for high-performance AI and machine learning workloads.
tagline: A high-density GPU accelerator platform featuring dual NVIDIA H100 graphics processors and massive system memory for advanced machine learning workloads.
bullet_points: Dual NVIDIA Hopper H100 GPUs with 160 GB total VRAM for parallel computing
24 Intel Xeon vCPUs with 480 GB of system memory
Top-tier performance in LLM inference and single-threaded compression
Shared CPU allocation on KVM virtualization
No local storage included, requiring external storage attachment
Best suited for deep learning, LLM execution, and data science
